info.title: Pinakes Mobile API
info.description: Versioned REST/JSON API for the Pinakes mobile companion app. Every response uses the `{data, meta, error}` envelope. Authenticated endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` obtained via POST /auth/login.

bearerAuth.description: Device-scoped, revocable opaque bearer token. Obtain via POST /auth/login. Store only the sha256 hash is kept server-side; compare with hash_equals.

Envelope.description: Standard API envelope. Every response is one of these two shapes.

data.description: Payload on success, null on error.

code.description: Machine-readable error code, e.g. invalid_credentials, app_disabled, rate_limited.

message.description: Human-readable message in the instance locale. Safe to display; never leaks internals.

MetaObject.description: Optional metadata. For search results includes next_cursor and total_count.

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. Null when no further pages.

total_count.description: Total matching records (search only, may be null on large sets).

https.description: Health endpoint: whether the connection is HTTPS.

warning.description: Health endpoint: insecure_transport when not HTTPS.

device_id.description: Stable per-device identifier from the OS.

token.description: Plaintext bearer token — returned once, store securely.

is_current.description: True if this is the device making the request.

disponibile.description: True if at least one copy is currently loanable.

PersonalHistory.description: Authenticated user's relationship with this book.

has_read.description: The user has a returned (past) loan of this book.

has_reserved.description: The user has a pending/active reservation for this book.

has_wishlisted.description: This book is in the user's wishlist.

has_active_loan.description: The user currently has this book on loan.

stato.description: e.g. in_corso, concluso, in_scadenza, scaduto, prenotato, in_attesa.

start_date.description: Requested start date (ISO-8601). Defaults to today if omitted.

PushSubscribeRequest.description: Register a push endpoint. For UnifiedPush provide `endpoint` + optional `public_key`/`auth`. For FCM provide `registration_id`.

endpoint.description: UnifiedPush: the distributor-provided HTTPS endpoint URL.

public_key.description: UnifiedPush WebPush: base64url-encoded ECDH public key.

registration_id.description: FCM: the registration token from the FCM SDK.

loan_due.description: Notify when a loan is due soon.

loan_overdue.description: Notify when a loan is overdue.

reservation_ready.description: Notify when a reservation is ready for pick-up.

new_message.description: Notify on admin reply / new message.

book_available.description: Notify when a wishlisted/reserved book is available again.

quiet_start.description: Quiet hours start (HH:MM, instance time zone).

vapid_public_key.description: VAPID public key (base64url uncompressed P-256 point) to use as the Web Push / UnifiedPush applicationServerKey. Null when push is not set up.

Forbidden.description: App access disabled or action not allowed.

get.description: Public. Returns library identity, feature flags, app_access_enabled, registration_enabled, and whether the connection is HTTPS. The native app calls this first after the user types the instance URL.
Returns this OpenAPI document (JSON). Public, no token required.
Returns the Swagger UI HTML page, self-hosted. Public, no token required.

post.description: Validates credentials against the utenti table (same hashing as the web flow), issues a 256-bit opaque token, stores only its sha256 hash. Returns the plaintext token ONCE — store it securely. Throttled: 10 attempts / 5 min per IP.
Available only when the instance registration toggle is on. Reuses the exact web INSERT + email-verification flow. Throttled: 5 attempts / hour per IP.
Sends a password-reset email using the same web reset flow. Always returns 200 to prevent email enumeration. Throttled: 5 attempts / hour per IP.

200.description: If the address exists, a reset email has been sent.

post.description: Sets `revoked_at` on the token presented in the Authorization header. Subsequent calls with the same token return 401.

get.description: Returns all non-revoked, non-expired tokens for the authenticated user. Scoped to own devices only.

delete.description: Revokes a specific device token. Only the owning user can revoke their own devices.

get.description: Cursor-paginated, filtered catalog search. Soft-deleted books are never returned (`AND deleted_at IS NULL`). Personal history is NOT included here — use /catalog/books/{id} for that.

parameters.name: genre
parameters.description: Filter by genre ID (cascade: any level)

parameters.name: available
parameters.description: If true, return only books with at least one loanable copy

get.description: Full book payload: availability, copies, shelf/location, absolute cover URL, full metadata, + personal history (has the user read/reserved/wishlisted it). Soft-deleted books return 404.
Full genre tree (up to 3 levels) for building filter UI dropdowns in the app.
Returns pending requests, active loans (scheduled / holding / overdue), and recent concluded history. Scoped to the authenticated user only.

post.description: Honors existing overlap, availability, and max-active-loans rules (same as the web form). Returns error codes for overlap, unavailable, or queue position.

delete.description: Cancels a pending reservation or pending loan request. Only the owning user can cancel their own reservation.

409.description: Cannot cancel a reservation already picked up / concluded.

post.description: Same as the web contact form: the message is stored (Flamingo) and an email is sent to the library manager.

get.description: Derived feed of loan/reservation/message events. This is the fallback when push notifications are off or not configured.

post.description: Registers a UnifiedPush or FCM endpoint for the current device. Always accepted even if the instance has no push credentials (NullProvider graceful fallback).

delete.description: Removes the push endpoint(s) associated with the current device token.

put.description: Per-type toggles + quiet hours. Partial update: only send the keys you want to change.
